paper_title: Adsorption Kinetic Study of Cesium on Natural Diatomite

methods.description: This method investigates the kinetics of cesium adsorption onto natural diatomite, examining how contact time and concentration affect the adsorption process. The study aims to understand the equilibrium and efficiency of cesium removal under different conditions.

steps.description: Various concentrations of non-radioactive CsNO3 ranging from 2.07 mg/l to 21.47 mg/l were used as carrier for Cs.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare solutions with varying concentrations of CsNO3

steps.description: Adsorption of Cs (I) increased very quickly at the initial contact time. After 60 minutes from beginning of the adsorption process the maximum amount of cesium adsorbed was achieved.
steps.searchable_summary: measure initial adsorption rate and maximum adsorption after 60 minutes

steps.description: In the next stage of the process (for 100 to 120 minutes after start), we could observe desorption (about 25%) of cesium previously adsorbed by the diatomite.
steps.searchable_summary: measure desorption rate between 100 to 120 minutes

steps.description: Cesium adsorption slowly increased with time reaching a plateau after 250-300 min of the process duration.

The minimum value of the Cs immobilization effect (62%) was for 21.47 mg/l of Cs(NO3) solution in acidic media while the greatest immobilization effect (96.9 %) was in alkaline media (with 2.07 mg/l Cs(NO3) concentration).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze immobilization effect in acidic vs alkaline media
